title.source: Collecting input and feedback (List, Map and Feed view)
title.target: Collecting input and feedback (List, Map and Feed view)

body:0001.source: FThis article is machine translated from english
body:0001.target: FThis article is machine translated from english

body:0002.source: What is the ‘Collect input and feedback’ method?
body:0002.target: What is the ‘Collect input and feedback’ method?

body:0003.source: Collecting input and feedback is the core participation method on the Go Vocal platform.
body:0003.target: Collecting input and feedback is the core participation method on the Go Vocal platform.

body:0005.source: Gather ideas or questions from residents (bottom‑up input)
body:0005.target: Gather ideas or questions from residents (bottom‑up input)

body:0006.source: Share options generated by your team and collect feedback (top‑down input)
body:0006.target: Share options generated by your team and collect feedback (top‑down input)

body:0007.source: Inputs can be displayed in two ways:
body:0007.target: Inputs can be displayed in two ways:

body:0008.source: List view: A scrollable, filterable list of inputs (with options for voting, commenting, and sorting)
body:0008.target: List view: A scrollable, filterable list of inputs (with options for voting, commenting, and sorting)

body:0009.source: Map view: Inputs pinned to locations for geospatial context
body:0009.target: Map view: Inputs pinned to locations for geospatial context

body:0010.source: Feed view: inputs are groups in main themes in post-it design
body:0010.target: Feed view: inputs are groups in main themes in post-it design

body:0011.source: The method is fully customizable, from what you call inputs (“ideas,” “options,” “questions,” etc.), to how they’re collected, displayed, and sorted.
body:0011.target: The method is fully customizable, from what you call inputs (“ideas,” “options,” “questions,” etc.), to how they’re collected, displayed, and sorted.

body:0012.source: For a more in-depth guide on map-specific capabilities on our platform, see our article:
body:0012.target: For a more in-depth guide on map-specific capabilities on our platform, see our article:

body:0013.source: Map Consultations and Using the ESRI ArcGIS mapping feature
body:0013.target: Map Consultations and Using the ESRI ArcGIS mapping feature

body:0014.source: Choosing how to collect and display inputs
body:0014.target: Choosing how to collect and display inputs

body:0017.source: Best for general idea collection, option feedback, and discussions
body:0017.target: Best for general idea collection, option feedback, and discussions

body:0018.source: Supports thumbs up/down voting and comments
body:0018.target: Supports thumbs up/down voting and comments

body:0026.source: Best for geographically specific input (e.g. urban planning, mobility)
body:0026.target: Best for geographically specific input (e.g. urban planning, mobility)

body:0027.source: Users pin ideas directly on a map
body:0027.target: Users pin ideas directly on a map

body:0028.source: Participants click a location on the map to drop a pin, then submit their idea there (the map does not appear in the form so they need to use the map on the project page to pin their ideas on a map)
body:0028.target: Participants click a location on the map to drop a pin, then submit their idea there (the map does not appear in the form so they need to use the map on the project page to pin their ideas on a map)

body:0029.source: Supports adding map layers for context (e.g. zones, infrastructure)
body:0029.target: Supports adding map layers for context (e.g. zones, infrastructure)

body:0034.source: ⚠️ ****The location question in the input form must remain enabled for map view to work. When you put the question on optional and users don’t fill in the location question, no pin on the map will appear.
body:0034.target: ⚠️ ****The location question in the input form must remain enabled for map view to work. When you put the question on optional and users don’t fill in the location question, no pin on the map will appear.

body:0037.source: Best for large amount of inputs collected and a need to group ideas in themes in real-time
body:0037.target: Best for large amount of inputs collected and a need to group ideas in themes in real-time

body:0038.source: Users can easily browse through the main themes, sub-themes and the individual submissions within it and can comment on start discussions on any of them.
body:0038.target: Users can easily browse through the main themes, sub-themes and the individual submissions within it and can comment on start discussions on any of them.

body:0039.source: This helps both residents and administrators make sense of large-scale participatory data, allowing for better engagement and decision-making
body:0039.target: This helps both residents and administrators make sense of large-scale participatory data, allowing for better engagement and decision-making

body:0044.source: Set up the themes/sub-themes analysis as described below
body:0044.target: Set up the themes/sub-themes analysis as described below

body:0045.source: Setting Up Themes/Sub-themes Analysis for the Perspective View:
body:0045.target: Setting Up Themes/Sub-themes Analysis for the Perspective View:

body:0051.source: Delete existing default tags, as the AI will create accurate input tags for you automatically
body:0051.target: Delete existing default tags, as the AI will create accurate input tags for you automatically

body:0053.source: Remove the Tags question type from the input form:
body:0053.target: Remove the Tags question type from the input form:

body:0054.source: Go to Timeline tab > Click on phase > Input Form tab > Edit Input Form > Delete Tags question.
body:0054.target: Go to Timeline tab > Click on phase > Input Form tab > Edit Input Form > Delete Tags question.

body:0056.source: Add or edit your themes/sub-themes by adjusting the current tags list.
body:0056.target: Add or edit your themes/sub-themes by adjusting the current tags list.

body:0057.source: Ensure the Tags question type is added to the input form:
body:0057.target: Ensure the Tags question type is added to the input form:

body:0058.source: Go to Timeline tab > Click on phase > Input Form tab > Edit Input Form > Ensure Tags question is included.
body:0058.target: Go to Timeline tab > Click on phase > Input Form tab > Edit Input Form > Ensure Tags question is included.

body:0059.source: How to customize the input experience?
body:0059.target: How to customize the input experience?

body:0061.source: Phrase participation in the right tone (e.g. “Submit a question” vs. “Post an idea”)
body:0061.target: Phrase participation in the right tone (e.g. “Submit a question” vs. “Post an idea”)

body:0062.source: You can find the terminology in your Phase settings → Setup → What should an input be called?
body:0062.target: You can find the terminology in your Phase settings → Setup → What should an input be called?

body:0064.source: Alternatives: Option, Project, Question, Issue, Contribution, Proposal, Initiative, Petition, Comment, Response, Suggestion, Topic, Post, Story
body:0064.target: Alternatives: Option, Project, Question, Issue, Contribution, Proposal, Initiative, Petition, Comment, Response, Suggestion, Topic, Post, Story

body:0066.source: You can find the form in your Phase settings → Input form → Edit input form
body:0066.target: You can find the form in your Phase settings → Input form → Edit input form

body:0068.source: Title (always required, cannot be removed and title name can not be edited)
body:0068.target: Title (always required, cannot be removed and title name can not be edited)

body:0070.source: Add extra questions (visible to admins/project managers only).
body:0070.target: Add extra questions (visible to admins/project managers only).

body:0071.source: Drag and drop text, images, or videos to add context before submission.
body:0071.target: Drag and drop text, images, or videos to add context before submission.

body:0072.source: ⚠️ For map consultations, do not remove the Location question, this ensures location data is collected.
body:0072.target: ⚠️ For map consultations, do not remove the Location question, this ensures location data is collected.

body:0073.source: Configure sorting and filtering of inputs
body:0073.target: Configure sorting and filtering of inputs

body:0083.source: 💡 Admins can set a default sorting method; users can change it via dropdown.
body:0083.target: 💡 Admins can set a default sorting method; users can change it via dropdown.

body:0084.source: ⚠️ Custom manual ordering is not possible.
body:0084.target: ⚠️ Custom manual ordering is not possible.

body:0086.source: When users start typing a title or description, similar existing ideas are shown
body:0086.target: When users start typing a title or description, similar existing ideas are shown

body:0087.source: This nudges users to engage in an existing discussion rather than posting duplicates
body:0087.target: This nudges users to engage in an existing discussion rather than posting duplicates

body:0088.source: Users can still submit their idea, this feature simply prompts reflection
body:0088.target: Users can still submit their idea, this feature simply prompts reflection

body:0089.source: To activate: toggle Similar Input Detection in the Phase settings
body:0089.target: To activate: toggle Similar Input Detection in the Phase settings

body:0091.source: Deciding what actions users can take
body:0091.target: Deciding what actions users can take

body:0092.source: Go to your phase Setup tab and scroll down to “Actions for users”. Here you can toggle whether participants can submit posts, comments, or react (like/dislike).
body:0092.target: Go to your phase Setup tab and scroll down to “Actions for users”. Here you can toggle whether participants can submit posts, comments, or react (like/dislike).

body:0093.source: Optionally disable dislikes or set vote limits per participant
body:0093.target: Optionally disable dislikes or set vote limits per participant

body:0094.source: If for example you toggle off “submitting posts” then normal users will see a button 'see ideas' instead 'submit idea' on the project page. Be aware that as an Admin or Project manager you will still see “submit ideas” because you are excluded from all these settings. You will still be able to submit inputs or comments even if it’s toggled off. Admins and Project managers can still add submissions after a phase has ended.
body:0094.target: If for example you toggle off “submitting posts” then normal users will see a button 'see ideas' instead 'submit idea' on the project page. Be aware that as an Admin or Project manager you will still see “submit ideas” because you are excluded from all these settings. You will still be able to submit inputs or comments even if it’s toggled off. Admins and Project managers can still add submissions after a phase has ended.

body:0095.source: Deciding who can take those actions
body:0095.target: Deciding who can take those actions

body:0096.source: In the phase Phase access and user data tab you can decide who is able to take the actions you set in the phase Setup tab. Only the options of actions users can take that you have turned on in the phase Setup tab will appear in the Phase access and user data tab for you to decided who can take those actions.
body:0096.target: In the phase Phase access and user data tab you can decide who is able to take the actions you set in the phase Setup tab. Only the options of actions users can take that you have turned on in the phase Setup tab will appear in the Phase access and user data tab for you to decided who can take those actions.

body:0097.source: Who can submit inputs? Which users can add ideas
body:0097.target: Who can submit inputs? Which users can add ideas

body:0098.source: Who can comment? Which users can add comments on ideas
body:0098.target: Who can comment? Which users can add comments on ideas

body:0099.source: Who can react? Which users can like or dislike ideas
body:0099.target: Who can react? Which users can like or dislike ideas

body:0100.source: Who can sign up for events? Which users can register for events linked to a phase
body:0100.target: Who can sign up for events? Which users can register for events linked to a phase

body:0101.source: For full details, see our article Understanding Access Rights.
body:0101.target: For full details, see our article Understanding Access Rights.
